name: Annual PM2.5 Concentrations for Countries and Urban Areas, v1 (1998–2016)
description: The Annual PM2.5 Concentrations for Countries and Urban Areas, 1998-2016, consists of mean concentrations of particulate matter (PM2.5) for countries and urban areas derived from Global Annual PM2.5 Grids.

creator.name: Center for International Earth Science Information Network (CIESIN)

provider.name: Geospatial Digital Special Collections (GDSC), University of Miami

additionalProperty.name: dataRepresentation
additionalProperty.description: Indicates the primary spatial data model.

distribution.name: PostgreSQL Dump
distribution.description: SQL dump for database restoration and querying.

variableMeasured.name: urbid
variableMeasured.description: Code of the urban extent polygons (not unique)

variableMeasured.name: stndrdname
variableMeasured.description: Name of the Urban Extent, caps and concatenated. If the urban extents polygons include more than one settlement, the name of the urban extent is the name of the settlement with the highest population

variableMeasured.name: name
variableMeasured.description: Name of the Urban Extent. If the urban extents polygons include more than one settlement, the name of the urban extent is the name of the settlement with the highest population

variableMeasured.name: isourbid
variableMeasured.description: Unique code of the urban extent polygons. It is created by concatenation of country ISO code and URBID

variableMeasured.name: sqkm
variableMeasured.description: Area of the urban extent polygon

variableMeasured.name: iso3
variableMeasured.description: 3 letter country code

variableMeasured.name: unsdcode
variableMeasured.description: UNSD country numerical code

variableMeasured.name: countryeng
variableMeasured.description: Country name

variableMeasured.name: continent
variableMeasured.description: Continent Name

variableMeasured.name: avpmu_1998
variableMeasured.description: PM2.5 Exposure for named urban areas from GRUMPv1: Urban Extent Polygons, Revision 02, from 1998. It is measured in micrograms per cubic meter, and is the average annual concentration of PM2.5 to which the typical citizen of each urban area is exposed. Blanks (-9999) represent missing data.
